[[VIDEOID:c049c5d22edce6fa891d4093e384169f]] THE most beautiful shoe I have ever owned! I have a variety of unique shoes (most especially animal face shoes) from mice, to cats, pandas, and puppies. These flats have got to be my favorite above them all! The quality of the shoes are AMAZING! The flats are sturdy and so far, they haven't created any bent marks from the start of the toes, to the end of them. The animal face stitching is stiff, which is a good thing, in my opinion. No loose pieces of thread sticking out from either side. The animal face patch/stitching doesn't hit you on the inside of the shoe. There's this fabric on the opposite side that protect the top of your foot from the stitching itself. The inside of the shoe is just as kawaii as the outside. It's branded, "Cute to the Core" and the sole is surrounded by gold trimming. So pretty and so effortless done. I have wide (W) feet. I have somewhat of an arch, but not enough to where I could fit a regular standard shoe comfortably. These flats accommodate regardless. There's great support and I've yet to get any blisters or have run in to areas of the flats that are too tight or too snug. The underside of the shoe (this is specifically for people with wide feet) or the outsole basically... Is the only one that touches the ground. I have had and still have many flats where the "vamp" or the edges of the shoes have touched the ground as well, because my feet are wide. These shoes, however, have not. I have worn them a total of 6 (six) times in 6 different occasions. I have received countless amounts of compliments! I wear character stockings with anything midi or mini so I have no experience wearing these flats barefoot. With stockings on, however, they fit perfectly and molded to my feet charmingly and comfortably. The only concern with these shoes that I have, is that they run a half size too small. I am a true 8 1/2 (US WOMEN'S) and I have had my feet sized with a Brannock Device at multiple shoe stores. Is this a problem to me, personally? No. As a first time buyer? It was a little concerning, but thanks to all of the reviews pointing this out, I'm glad I overcame the uncertainty. So for those who are in the "half" sizes, I do suggest sizing UP. My "Cute to the Core" animal head flats came in a black shoe box. Even the shoe box is stylish and trendy! Overall, my experience with these shoes have been an amazing one! It isn't so often that I find shoes or flats in general that fit me well, and fit me comfortably. These flats hit every positive check mark on my list of what to look for in a shoe. On top of the stylish and fashionable look these flats carry throughout every trend season, they are, "100% VEGAN LEATHER: These premium flats are an earth-conscious product" (as stated in the product description. This claim is not on the box though. The only information on the shoe box were the size, color, and style.) There was no product guarantee in the product description during the time of my purchase. EDIT: I've tried to visit their website to add references in my review but it is currently unavailable at the moment.

I'm 5'-2", 127 pounds and ordered the small in the distressed jeans. They fit perfectly and are very flattering. They are a tad shorter than what I am used to wearing, but I figured what the heck! This was a great purchase.  Don't pay those very high prices at "vendor" just for a band.  They have only two styles!  Aluminum and stainless steel case, that's it, nothing else.  All interior workings are the same. Extra pricing is just for bands.  I purchased this very sharp band, took it to a watch repair, as it was difficult of me to take out links. It comes with two sturdy tools to take out the link, see box photo, but still it was easier to take it to a watch repair person.  He took out three links in a minute, and it fit perfect.  He didn't want to charge, so I tipped him. As far as changing bands?  Apple has made in incredibly simple.  Your finger easily pushes the almost hidden button under the band and the band slides off easily sideways, as does this band slide back on easily.  I've already received compliments, within hours of wearing the new band.

So extremely comfortable, high waisted (if you went up a size for comfort) and textured. I love the feeling on the inside. It leaves no lints after the first wear and after the first wash and dry process. Leaves no "cotton" residue in between the thigh area and is cuffed comfortably at the ankles. There are zipper pockets that I think look best when they're closed. The thickness and quality of these joggers are pretty on point. I have worn this to the airport, out and about and at home. Same level of comfort experience no matter where I go. I've yet to get any type of allergic reaction to the texture or the fabric. Bonus was when it came in without that warehouse smell. I usually receive (items online IN GENERAL) my purchases with a nail polish or paint thinner smell to them. This had no smell what so ever. Overall, I could have ordered true to size and it would still fit perfectly. I did size up just for comfort. I like the loose fit I get when I wear joggers. There was no product guarantee in the product description during the time of my purchase.

My first attempt was a plastic box that looked good until I tried to put dangly earrings in it. They didn't fit. So, I took it back and decided to check Amazon to see what they had. [[VIDEOID:c00536a031089a270c567f676bf9a934]] These shoes are a size 9. I am a size 8 1/2 wide (W). If you generally wear a wide like I do, and are a half size, I would suggest sizing up. The width of the shoe is definitely not the problem. The arch is so minimal, I think those who do have an arch would find these slip on shoes somewhat too big (in my opinion). The only concern I have with these slip on shoes is the material folded on the inside of the shoe, underneath the seam. This is located at the heel. The seam is folded inward and a very small piece of fabric beneath the seam irritates my skin with or without socks. The fold of that panel lifts while I am mobile. Whenever I lift my feet off the group, the panel follows. This irritates my skin to where it feels like a blister will form. My forth attempt in these shoes, I had to switch out. The shoes hurt my heel too much. There was too much friction. I'm looking for ways to minimize this, so if you have any suggestions as to what I could do, I will appreciate them. Other than that, the fit is extremely comfortable. The sole of the shoe is about an inch and a half in length, and will add height so keep that in mind. The cat print on the front side of the shoe is even more pigmented in person. The video I provided for references does the cat print no justice. There is no irritation on the inside of the shoe behind the tongue or the elastic. The fold you see in the front of my shoe is from walking. It really depends how you walk if you're worried about a "fold" creating in the front of the slip on shoe (the fold is not from the shoe being tight. like I said it is comfortable and loosely fitted in the slightest, especially for someone who wears a wide (W), generally). Overall, besides the panel lifting from beneath the seam inside the shoe, everything else seems to be spot on and perfect! I love these shoes, and YRU never disappoints when it comes to beautiful and unique shoes.
There was no product guarantee during the time of my purchase. EDIT: Please refer to the video for more information on these shoes.
